reasoning: While the fidelity scores are exceptionally high (0.9959), the identified concerns involve specific factual and numeric errors in the 'QUICK FACTS' section (e.g., '6,00' instead of '6,000' and 'CLJEADS' instead of 'CJLEADS'). In the context of North Carolina government documents, numeric data integrity is vital; these errors represent objective content failures that outweigh the high composite score and require manual correction.

concerns.description: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
concerns.analysis: Two minor text differences were identified in the 'QUICK FACTS' section. First, the number of healthcare facilities in onboarding is listed as '6,00' instead of the correct '6,000' shown in the source. Second, the acronym for Criminal Justice Law Enforcement Automated Data Services is listed as 'CLJEADS' instead of the correct 'CJLEADS'. These are objective errors that alter specific data points.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: 9,000+ healthcare facilities linked to NC HealthConnex; another 6,000 in onboarding ... accessed Criminal Justice Law Enforcement Automated Data Services (CJLEADS)
concerns.output_quote: 9,000+ healthcare facilities linked to NC HealthConnex; another 6,00 in onboarding ... accessed Criminal Justice Law Enforcement Automated Data Services (CLJEADS)
concerns.location: QUICK FACTS section, list items 6 and 10 (element index 8)

action_items: Correct the number of healthcare facilities in onboarding to '6,000'.
Fix the acronym for Criminal Justice Law Enforcement Automated Data Services to 'CJLEADS'.
